series_metadata.series_description: Audits and reviews performed by or on behalf of an agency, including the working papers that support the audit. Also includes audits performed on the agency.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
The State Auditor's Office retains
any copies of its audits performed
on Texas state agencies.

series_metadata.series_description: Including any supporting documentation created and/or used to justify and support legislative appropriations requests by an agency.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_metadata.series_description: Complaints and other unsolicited feedback received by an agency from the public concerning the agency and records pertaining to the resolution of complaints.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes
the subject of litigation, it must be
included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item
number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence pertaining to the formulation, planning, implementation, interpretation, modification, or redefinition of the programs, services, or projects of an agency and the administrative regulations, policies, and procedures that govern them.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
administrative correspondence of
executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: This records series and
item number 1.1.008 should be
used only for correspondence that
is not included in or directly related
to another records series on the
agency's approved schedule. For
example, a memorandum that
documents an appropriations
request must be retained for the
minimum retention period
prescribed by item number 1.1.004;
a letter concerning an audit for that
prescribed by item number 1.1.002,
etc. SEE ALSO item number 1.1.011.

series_metadata.series_description: Non-administrative incoming/outgoing and internal correspondence, in any media, pertaining to or arising from the routine operations of the policies, programs, services, or projects of an agency.

tracking_data.comments:
See comment to item number
1.1.007. SEE ALSO item number
1.1.010.

series_metadata.series_description: Any document that officially initiates, rescinds, or amends general office procedures.
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that governs the programs, services, or projects of an agency.

Calendars, appointment books or programs and scheduling, or itinerary records, purchased with state funds or maintained by staff during business hours that document appointments, itineraries, and other activities of agency officials or employees.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds, but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General.

series_metadata.series_description: News, press releases, or any public relations files maintained or issued by an agency. Includes print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual records.
Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are furnished to the public under Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes all correspondence and documentation relating to requests for records that are excepted under the Public Information Act (Chapter 552, Government Code), including records provided to the legislature under section 552.008.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Charts or diagrams that show the structure of an agency and the relationships and relative rank of its departments and job positions.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the Archives and Information
Services Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Plans and records relating to the process of planning new or redefined programs, services, or projects of an agency that are not included in or directly related to other records series in this schedule.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or any other documents required by law to be submitted to the Texas Register.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Drafts of proposed legislation and related correspondence.
Surveys returned by the customers or clients of an agency, and the statistical data maintained rating an agency's performance.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
See item number 1.1.067 for
summary reports compiled from
customer surveys.

series_metadata.series_description: Notes or text of speeches, papers, presentations, or reports delivered in conjunction with agency work.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors and program
heads require archival review.

series_metadata.series_description: Instructional materials developed by an agency for training entities or individuals non-personnel, it regulates or serves, including class rosters, sign-in sheets, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records created by or on behalf of an agency in anticipation of or in the adjudication of a lawsuit.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set
legal precedent or exhibit historical
value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services
Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission for
archival preservation. See page ix
of the State Records Retention
Schedule for more information.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records used to register persons appearing before state agencies as required by Chapter 2004, Government Code, including quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics Commission.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Information resources and operational strategic plans prepared in accordance with 2054.095 and 2056.002, Government Code. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating the final plan.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Self-evaluations and plans documenting compliance with the requirements of the Americans With Disabilities Act.

tracking_data.comments:
Records may be retained longer
than the minimum as
administratively valuable.

series_metadata.series_description: Records of temporary usefulness that are not an integral part of a records series of an agency, that are not regularly filed within an agency’s recordkeeping system, and that are required only for a limited period of time for the completion of an action by an official or employee of the agency or in the preparation of an ongoing records series. Transitory records are not essential to the fulfillment of statutory obligations or to the documentation of agency functions. Some examples of transitory information, which can be in any medium (voice mail, fax, email, hard copy, etc.) are routine messages; telephone message notifications; internal meeting notices; routing slips; incoming letters or memoranda of transmittal that add nothing of substance to enclosures; and similar routine information used for communication, but not for the documentation, of a specific agency transaction.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should use caution in
assigning this records series item
number to records of an agency to
make certain they are not part of
another records series listed in this
schedule or, for records series
unique to an agency, are not part of
a records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory
obligations of the agency or the
documentation of its functions. The
disposal of transitory information
need not be documented through
destruction signoffs (1.2.001) or in
records disposition logs (1.2.010),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of
these records as part of its records
management plan (1.2.014).

series_metadata.series_title: Meeting Agenda and Minutes of Open
series_metadata.series_description: Official agenda and minutes of state boards, committees, commissions, and councils that conduct open meetings as required by Government Code, Chapter 551.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The
archival requirement (see Texas
Government Code, Section
324.008(d)) will be met by sending
a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives
Commission. CAUTION: This
records series and item numbers
1.1.059, 1.1.060, 1.1.061, and
1.1.062 must be used for those
state boards, committees,
commissions, and councils, which
by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are
administered by another state
agency. These records and all
others related to the functions of
any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the
administering agency.

series_metadata.series_title: Meetings, Certified Agendas or Audiovisual Recordings of Closed
series_metadata.series_description: Certified agendas or audiovisual recordings of closed meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
See caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Audiovisual recordings of open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) 90 days

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Minutes of state
agencies are permanent records.
audiovisual recordings are not
permanent media. State agencies
may not retain audiovisual
recordings of the meetings of
governing bodies in lieu of written
minutes. The proceedings of all
meetings of state boards,
committees, commissions, and
councils must be reduced to
writing. SEE caution comment at
item number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Notes taken during open meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils from which written minutes are prepared.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) 90 days

tracking_data.comments:
See caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Documents submitted at meetings of state boards, commissions, committees, and councils including exhibit items, documentation for agenda items, etc. Includes documents sent in advance of meetings for briefing purposes, some of which may not be submitted at an actual meeting.

tracking_data.comments:
See caution comment at item
number 1.1.058.

series_metadata.series_description: Minutes or notes, and supporting documentation, taken at internal agency staff meetings.

Any records of an agency needed for the documentation of output, outcome, efficiency, and explanatory measures in an agency's appropriations request or strategic plan, and for performance measures used to manage the agency.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: The FE + 3 retention
period overrides any shorter
retention period for a records
series in this schedule if the records
series is needed for documentation
of agency performance measures.

series_metadata.series_title: Reports and Studies (Non- Fiscal) - Raw Data
series_metadata.series_description: Information or data collected and compiled for the purpose of producing non-fiscal reports.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include source
documentation used for
information or data included in or
directly related to another records
series in this schedule. SEE
especially item number 1.1.064.

series_metadata.series_description: Biennial narrative reports to the governor and legislature as required by an agency's enabling statutes, including annual narrative reports if they are required by statute.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3. The requirement is
met by sending the required copies
of the reports to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission. Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final report may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Annual, sub-annual, or special reports or studies on non-fiscal aspects of an agency's programs, services, or projects compiled by agency personnel, by advisory committees, or by consultants under contract with an agency that are not noted elsewhere in this schedule. Includes reports distributed either internally or to other entities.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are
archival when they deal with
significant aspects of the agency's
programs. For agencies that have
had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of
archival coding, A, R, or E. SEE page
ix of the State Records Retention
Schedule for more information.

series_metadata.series_description: Quarterly and annual reports on agency performance measures submitted to executive and legislative offices.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Reports compiled by agency personnel on a daily or other periodic basis pertaining to workload monitoring, task completion times, number of public contacts, and similar activities.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: SEE item number
1.1.064.

series_metadata.series_title: Agency Rules, Policies, and Procedures - Final
series_metadata.series_description: Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or similar records, and their working files used in development, distributed internally for the use of employees or externally to the public or those individuals or entities regulated by an agency that sets out the rules, policies, and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to
archival requirement and may be
disposed of at the expiration of the
retention period.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports made to the Office of the Attorney General on an agency's Public Information Act activities.
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and othe correspondence and supporting documentation related to the Sunset review process for state agencies.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission's draft
report and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations. The final version
of the agency self-evaluation report
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_metadata.series_description: Master versions of all forms used internally and externally by the agency, including subsequent revisions to an agency form or any associated design or design modification requests.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR 105) that identifies the records that are created and maintained by an agency, provides the minimum timeframes the records must be retained, and includes instructions for their disposition. This series may include working files and the documentation of certification and approval by the Texas State Library and Archives Commission.

tracking_data.comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by
the State and Local Records
Management Division, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copy (includes RMD 101, Tx-R-5, 306-58-1, and Agency Storage Forms). Forms used to track the transmittal of records to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a transfer of physical custody.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
AC = Date of authorization for
destruction, permanent transfer
from storage, or transfer to the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by the
agency records management
officer.

series_metadata.series_description: Logs or similar records listing records destroyed or transferred to the Archives and Information Services Division, Texas State Library and Archives Commission, showing records series title, dates of records, and date destroyed or transferred.
Includes indexes, card files, shelf lists, registers, guides, and other aids to provide access to records.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_metadata.series_description: Records management plans and similar records that establish the policies and procedures under which records and information are managed in an agency.
Also includes documentation for disaster recovery services provided by other entities.
Forms used by the agency or other entities to establish disaster recovery services, authorize agency staff to access the media, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: One copy of each state publication as defined on page xi of the State Records Retention Schedule, except a publication that is subject to a different retention period in this schedule. For example, a meeting agenda (see item number 1.1.058) also meets the definition, but it must be retained permanently; item numbers 1.1.004, 1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003, which also meet the definition, are closely associated with the appropriations process and must be retained AC + 6.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission, by law
(Government Code 441.101-
441.106). The Texas State Library
and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state
publications submitted to it on a
continuing basis, subject to periodic
evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
Internet, consult the administrative
rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of
the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (13 TAC §§3.1-
3.16).

series_metadata.series_description: Background material, copy (drafts), original artwork, photo negatives, prints, flats, etc. This includes all work performed both inside and outside the agency.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Successive and
substantive drafts of major
publications may be archival. Major
is defined by both the publication's
authoriship and its impact on Texas
and Texans. Original artwork,
including photo prints and
negatives that have significant
value as evidence of agency
programs as well as the potential
for re-use, may be archival.

series_metadata.series_description: Relatively long-lived computer files, including files used in the creation, utilization, and updating of master files, containing organized and consistent sets of complete and accurate electronic records. Examples include, but are not limited to, data tables of relational databases used by applications or computer programs.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Agency-developed automated software applications and operating system files including job control language, programs, applications, scripts, source code, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting operational and maintenance requirements of computer hardware such as operating manuals, hardware/operating system requirements, hardware configurations, equipment control systems, and associated processing files.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Records adequate to specify all technical characteristics necessary for reading or processing of electronic records and their timely, authorized disposition including documentation describing how a system operates and necessary for using the system such as user guides, system or sub- system definitions, system specifications, input and output specifications, and system flow charts; program descriptions and documentation such as program flowcharts, program maintenance logs, change notices, and other records that document modifications to computer programs; and data documentation necessary to access, retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an automated system such as a data element dictionary, file layout, code book or table, and other records that explain the meaning, purpose, structure, logical relationships, and origin of the data elements.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_metadata.series_description: Files needed for electronic data audits such as files or reports showing transactions accepted, rejected, suspended, and/or processed; history files/tapes; records of online updates to application files or security logs.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Hardware and software components for monitoring agency's computer system resources and performance.

Manuals, guidelines, or similar documents establishing data processing policies and procedures in an agency in such areas as access and security, systems development, data retention and disposition, data ownership, production control, system back-up, etc.

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
technical documentation of
procedures necessary for reading
or processing of electronic records.
SEE item number 2.1.009.

series_metadata.series_description: Forms and logs used to reconcile batches submitted for processing against batches received and processed.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: A record of web pages visited during an internet session, including data files of user-specific information created by the webserver, that allows users to access previously visited pages more quickly or to generate a record of usage of a state- owned computer.

series_metadata.series_title: Software Registrations, Warranties and License Agreements
series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the registration and licensing of a software application to activate the software for legal use by the end users of a state agency, along with warranties providing that the software will perform in accordance with functional specifications.

Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include ADA
Accommodation Requests.

series_metadata.series_description: Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of reference, and similar documents whose submission by candidates for vacant positions is required on the application form, by application procedures, or in the employment advertisement.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the counseling of an employee for work- related, personal, or substance abuse problems. Usually maintained at the supervisorial level except in those agencies with counseling staff.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Agency copies of information relating to the selection by employees of life, health, vision, dental, disability, and other types of insurance or benefits offered by the State of Texas to its employees.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Documents that serve as
payroll deduction authorizations
must be maintained for the
retention period prescribed for
item number 3.2.001.

series_metadata.series_description: Internal or external announcements or advertisements of job openings, promotions, training programs, or opportunities for overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after 9/1/15.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Includes a contract or agreement regarding the terms and conditions of employment of an individual and a contract with a vendor for temporary staffing services. Executed, renewed, or amended on or before 8/31/15.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Records relating to the review of employee grievances against personnel policies, working conditions, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaints filed by an agency
employee with the Equal
Employment Office (EEO) of the U.
S. Department of Labor. SEE item
number 1.1.048.

series_metadata.series_description: Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or other similar documents used to evaluate the performance of an employee.
Corrective actions are those actions which do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are imposed to correct or improve an employee's job performance.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: If, during the retention
period of this records series, any
part of this series is used to
document and support personnel
disciplinary action under item
number 3.1.021, all documentation
from this series used to support
disciplinary action must be
retained for the the minimum
retention period described by item
number 3.1.021.

series_metadata.series_description: Disciplinary actions are those actions which may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are imposed to discipline an employee whose conduct is harmful to the best interests of the state, the agency, or the employee work force; and for failure to improve performance or conduct following imposition of corrective action. May include cause for demotion, suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for failure to give written notice of resignation. May also be used to document evidence of employee self- improvement efforts, as well as favorable and unfavorable communication.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Forms or similar records used to create or change information concerning the records of an employee including pay grade, position classification, employee number, evaluation date, and termination of employment.
Job descriptions, including all associated task or skill statements, for positions in an agency.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Medical or physical examination reports or certificates of employees for whom periodic monitoring of health or fitness is required.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
preemployment physical
examinations. SEE item number
3.1.014.

series_metadata.series_description: Criminal history record information on job applicants or agency employees obtained from the Department of Public Safety (DPS).

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal
history record information from
DPS must refer to its agency's
legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for
appropriate retention and use of
this information.

series_metadata.series_description: Certificates of completion, transcripts, test scores, or similar records documenting the training, testing, or continuing education achievements of an employee.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_title: Employment Eligibility, Documentation or Verification of Federal reporting form (INS I-9).
series_metadata.series_description: Federal reporting form (I-9)

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Summary of applicant qualifications, evaluation basis for selection or rejection, original applications, job assignments, promotions, separations, layoffs, terminations, compensation records, conditions of work, time records, hours of training provided (separate from labor), minority status, and gender of all selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Form completed and signed by employee or official, or former employee or official, electing to keep home address, home telephone number, social security number, and family information open or confidential under the Public Information Act, Government Code §552.024.

tracking_data.comments:
See item number 3.3.011.
CAUTION: Most recent public
access election information must
be kept as long as the former
employee verification records.

series_metadata.series_description: Consultation records, notes, letters, memos, emails, reports and other documentation.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include formal
complaint filed with EEO. If matter
becomes a grievance or the subject
of counseling or litigation, or
employee is subject to disciplinary
action, the records are subject to
retention in the appropriate
records series. SEE 1.1.048,
3.1.006, 3.1.018, 3.1.020, and
3.1.021.

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary or required deductions from payroll, including garnishment or other court-ordered attachments.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_metadata.series_description: Payroll records and registers documenting employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may include but is not limited to payroll input records, summary statements, payroll vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll history.

Records and documentation relating to unemployment compensation claims, including reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission (TWC).

series_metadata.series_description: Affirmative action plans for both regular employees and apprenticeship programs.

series_metadata.series_description: Employee benefit plans such as pension; life, health, and disability insurance; deferred compensation; etc., including amendments.

series_metadata.series_description: Reports providing statistical information on labor force.
Records relating to review and monitoring of job classifications within an agency.
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or assignments.
Reports to the agency from TWC or its predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_metadata.series_title: Reimbursable Activities, Requests and Authorizations to Engage in
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for travel; participation in educational programs, workshops, or college classes; or for other work-related activities for which the expenses of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines, or similar records that define agency wide policies and procedures concerning the personnel of an agency.
Any documents detailing the procedural duties and responsibilities of agency positions on a position-by-position basis.

Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects of personnel staffing, including listings of all staff by program or name, staff hired during a month, detailed listings of employees within its organizational structure, position vacancies, analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of employment, etc.
Aptitude or skills tests required of job applicants or of current personnel to qualify for promotion or transfer, including records of the vailidation of aptitude and skills tests.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each
different test (different in terms of
either questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for
the period indicated.

series_metadata.series_description: Completed aptitude and skills test papers of job applicants or of current personnel taking a test to qualify for promotion or transfer.

Instructional materials and other records associated with in-house training of agency personnel on personnel policies and procedures and other policies and procedures that govern an agency's programs, services, or projects, includes class rosters, sign-in sheets, etc.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
hazardous material training
records. SEE item number 5.4.007.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes documentation used to complete EEO reports.
Reports, studies, aggregated or summary data, and similar documentation compiled to monitor and demonstrate compliance with the federal Equal Pay Act.

Used to create employee leave balances, to transfer leave balances when an employee transfers positions, to adjust carry-over balances on August 31, to correct errors on leave accumulation, and to close out leave accounts on separated employees.
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle and provides employee leave status information for each position.
Records created to schedule time worked by employees outside of or in addition to their regular working hours, including approval authorizations.

Records documenting individual employee's hours worked, including work schedules and documentation evidencing adherence to or deviation from normal hours for those employees working on fixed schedules.

series_metadata.series_title: Time Off and/or Sick Leave Requests
series_metadata.series_description: Requests and authorizations for vacation, compensatory, sick, Family and Medial Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of authorized leave, and supporting documentation.
Requests submitted, approvals, number of hours transferred in and out, etc.

Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks and purchase orders; expenditure authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance, purchase, general journal, expenditure, and special vouchers; and similar records that serve to document disbursements, including those documenting claims for and reimbursement to employees for travel and other expenses.

Records, including employee savings bond ledgers, documenting the investment of any public funds that evidence the investment of such funds, the cancellation or withdrawal of investments, and similar activities.
Required by the General Appropriations Act. Includes encumbrances and documentation about budget transfers and revisions, as well as detail charts of accounts.
Periodic reports compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).

series_metadata.series_description: Yearly report compiled from information entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
Records documenting the internal and external procedural requirements with respect to the accounting department of a state agency.
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible, including write-off authorizations and returned checks, warrants, and drafts.

series_metadata.series_description: Documentation relating to the capital and fixed assets of a state agency, including equipment or property history cards containing data on initial cost, depreciation schedules or summaries used for capital outlay budgeting, and property sale, auction, or disposal records of agency owned equipment and property. Including inventory and other cost files, production, job, labor, quotes, pricing, specification, etc.
Records documenting requests for payment of a sum of money according to the terms of a policy or contract.

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting financial obligations of a state agency that are not payable within one year of the date of the balance sheet, including debentures, loans, deferred tax liabilities, bonds, and pension obligations.

series_metadata.series_description: Signature cards or similar records establishing authority of an agency employee to initiate or authorize financial transactions on behalf of an agency.

series_metadata.series_description: Contracts, leases, and agreements include general obligation, land lease, utilities, and construction except for buildings. May also include other applicable documentation in the master contract file per Texas Comptroller of Public Accounts Contract Management Guide. Executed, renewed, or amended on or after 9/1/15.

building construction contracts and
item number 5.1.017 for contract
logs.

series_metadata.series_title: Disaster Preparedness and Recovery Plans Continuity of Operations Plans (COOP)
series_metadata.series_description: Plans developed (per Texas Labor Code, 412.054) to mitigate the damage of potential events that could endanger an agency’s ability to function. Includes disaster preparedness, response, and recovery plans.

Includes internal listing of incoming/outgoing agency telephone activity, long-distance facsimile or electronic transmission logs, summary details created by agencies for internal documentation purposes.

series_metadata.series_title: General Land Office Building Restoration Project Files
series_metadata.series_description: Project Development including surveys and reports, Design including meeting minutes and design development deliverables, Procurement, and Construction files, including RFI’s, ASI’s, Submittals, etc.
Files include research information related to businesses, foundations and individuals who were approached to donate to the Capitol Fund for the 1990s Capitol restoration.

tracking_data.comments: Archival Exemption 1/17/97

series_metadata.series_description: Materials documenting the state wide fundraising done in support of the money appropriated by the Legislature for the 1990s Capitol restoration.
Files transferred from TFC, including, but not limited to asbestos and hazardous waste projects for the Capitol.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

tracking_data.comments: Archival Exemption 1/17/97

series_metadata.series_description: Project Development including surveys and reports, Design including meeting minutes and design development deliverables, Procurement, and Construction files, including RFI’s, ASI’s, Submittals, etc.
Files transferred from TFC, including, but not limited to the final record of construction documents and Curatorial files for warehouse storage.

series_metadata.series_title: Texas State History Museum Project Files
series_metadata.series_description: Includes records relating to correspondence, projects summaries and reports, drawings and specifications related to design and construction, marketing, fundraising, contracts, and interagency agreements related to facilities, exhibitions, programs, and museum-related initiatives beginning in 1998.
Records relating to annual donations, sponsorhips, and revenue generated through retail, parking admission for exhibitions, theaters, prorams, and third- party commissions.
Information about Capitol Events, includes but not limited to working files, correspondence, and related documentation for events.
Construction and General Land Office Building & Grounds Restoration Payment Vouchers help to chronicle the details of the renovation process.
Construction and General Land Office Building & Grounds Restoration Contracts help to document the specifics of what was done during the renovation.

series_metadata.series_title: Status of the Capitol Collections Annual Report to the Board
series_metadata.series_description: Report on the status of the Capitol Collections, showing the evolution of the collection over time.

tracking_data.comments:
Since the Collections Review
Committee (CRC) was disbanded in
2005, the Capitol Collection
information is provided at each
board meeting.

series_metadata.series_description: Includes Capitol facility history, including but not limited to building change requests, facility rules, updates, and general-focused maintenance documents.

Includes, but is not limited to information about various Capitol grounds elements that do not have Capitol Historical Artifact (CHA) status but are an important part of the Grounds historical record.

Files include, but are not limited to, information about the Capitol Historical Artifact (CHA) zinc Goddess of Liberty 1989.652 (now on display at BTSHM) as well as the aluminum replica that was fabricated and placed in 1986.

Includes, but is not limited to information about past Capitol Events and Exhibits, such as the Capitol Centennial Celebration in 1988, as well as the Capitol Restoration Celebration in 1995 in which Curatorial staff was involved with their creation or implementation.
Includes, but is not limited to information about the research and creation of the Captiol Historical Furnishings Project during the 1990s building restoration.
Correspondence related to the development of the General Land Office Building.
Information about education programs including tours at the CVC since its public opening in 1994.
Information about exhibits at the CVC since its public opening in 1994.
Policies, procedures or similar records at the CVC since its 1994 public opening. Includes working files and related documentation used in creating rules, policies, and procedures.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final rules, policies, and
procedures, are not subject to archival
requirement and may be disposed of
at the expiration of the retention
period.

series_metadata.series_description: Information about special events including building rental at the CVC since its 1994 public opening.
Information about the history and 1990s restoration of the General Land Office Building (GLOB).
Information about the General Land Office Building (GLOB) legislation and legal opinions related to its use after 1919 (DRT and UDC arrival).
Reports related to the development of the General Land Office Building.
Information about any publications produced in support of the CVC since its 1994 public opening.

tracking_data.comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: These
publications are sent directly to
the State Archives rather than the
State Publications Department

series_metadata.series_description: Records documenting the request for and response to help desk tickets received by divisions or units, such as information technology.

Agreements between employee and agency authorizing certain actions, including acknowledgement forms, telecommuting agreements, outside/secondary employment authorizations, or other documentation that show proof of receipt and awareness of and adherence to agency policies and procedures.

Bank statements, credit card statements, cancelled checks, check registers, deposit slips, debit and credit notices, reconcilliations, and other banking related records.
